title: Virtual Machines
subtitle: QEMU/KVM — bridged networking, VNC in browser, snapshots.
tip_iso: upload an ISO via the ISO Library first, then create a VM with that ISO.

imports.title: Disk Import
imports.server_path_placeholder: Server path to existing disk (admin only, e.g. /tmp/disk.qcow2)
imports.delete_confirm_done: (qcow2 will also be deleted if not used by a VM)

logs.no_log: No log file yet — VM has not been started.

create.title: Create new VM
create.error_name_invalid: Name: 1–32 chars, starts with letter, only a-z A-Z 0-9 -
create.field_name_hint: 1–32 chars, starts with letter, only a-z A-Z 0-9 -
create.field_import_hint: qcow2 will be moved into the VM; the job disappears after.
create.no_isos: No ISOs available — upload one first.
create.disk_size_from_import: Disk size comes from the imported file.
create.boot_iso_desc: New disk + ISO as boot medium
create.boot_blank_desc: Boot loop until setup is provided
create.net_bridged_desc: VM gets DHCP IP from LAN
create.disk_hint_iso: virtio = fastest performance under KVM. sata when guest OS lacks virtio drivers (e.g. Windows without virtio-win).
create.disk_hint_import: sata = compatible with most imported images. virtio if the source includes virtio drivers.
create.machine_hint: q35 = modern ICH9 chipset (default). pc = i440FX, compatible with FreeBSD ZFS boot, Windows XP, old Linux, VirtualBox imports.
create.netdev_hint: virtio-net-pci = fast (default). e1000 = Intel NIC, supported in almost every guest OS — for imports without virtio drivers.

edit.error_name_invalid: Name: 1-32 chars, starts with letter, only a-z A-Z 0-9 -
